_description: Metric catalog for Bob Frm Mktg. Defines what we optimize for, the full acquisition funnel, and every metric formula. Read by agents for context; edit primary_goal_context to reflect why in_app_conversions matters for your app.
primary_goal_context: We optimize for in-app conversion events (e.g. first order, signup completion) because install volume alone doesn't predict revenue. CPA is therefore the lead efficiency metric; cost and installs are supporting signals.

significance_thresholds.note: Any of these triggers auto-escalation to delta diagnosis without waiting for user to ask

metrics.label: Users
metrics.formula: metrics.unique_users (Google Ads API — not additive across networks)
metrics.description: Unique users who saw at least one ad in the period.

metrics.label: CPM
metrics.description: Cost per thousand impressions. Lower = more efficient buying.

metrics.label: Frequency
metrics.description: Average number of times each unique user saw an ad. High frequency can signal saturation.

metrics.label: CTR %
metrics.description: Click-through rate. Low CTR often points to creative or audience relevance issues.

metrics.label: CTI %
metrics.description: Click-to-install rate. Measures post-click landing page + store page quality.

metrics.label: Conversions
metrics.description: The primary goal metric — the number that drives bid optimization.

metrics.label: Conv %
metrics.description: Conversion rate from click. Combines CTI and post-install conversion quality.

metrics.label: CPA
metrics.description: Cost per acquisition (primary goal event). The lead efficiency metric for bid decisions. Lower = better.

metrics.label: CPI
metrics.description: Cost per install. Secondary efficiency metric — always based on installs, regardless of primary_goal. Useful when install volume matters alongside post-install conversion quality.

zero_denominator_policy: All ratio metrics return 'NA' when the denominator is zero. Never return NaN or 0 for ratios.
do_not_select_reason: Pre-computed API averages are averages-of-averages when aggregated across a period. Always recalculate from raw sums (clicks, impressions, cost).
